title: Orchestrator CLIs validate items against the item-subschema FRAGMENT in isolation (losing $ref definitions), not whole-file like block-api
description: file-block-item.ts and append-relation.ts (the dual-surface append CLIs) re-implement pre-write item validation against the block's ITEMS SUBSCHEMA FRAGMENT extracted from the schema (file-block-item.ts:109 itemSchema=properties[arrayKey].items, validated at :270; append-relation.ts:117-139 extracts relations .items, validated at :157), rather than validating the whole file the way the library does (appendToBlock->writeBlock->writeTypedFile->validateFromFile against the WHOLE schema, block-api.ts:401 — proven to resolve $refs). When an items schema is {"$ref":"#/definitions/<kind>"} (features.schema.json:10, spec-reviews.schema.json:38), the fragment cannot resolve #/definitions/<kind> (parent definitions out of scope) -> --dry-run errors "can't resolve reference". ACTIVE on file-block-item filing features/spec-reviews; LATENT in append-relation (relations.schema.json has no $ref today but the same fragment-in-isolation pattern is shipped). NOTE: the REAL (non-dry-run) file-block-item append delegates straight to appendToBlock and is NOT broken — only --dry-run (this gap) + --auto-id (FGAP-083) fail. Conception (samples) + registry schemas use no item $ref, so .context library filing is unaffected. Root: orchestrator-CLI validation is a PARALLEL re-implementation diverging from block-api whole-file validation (feedback_no_parallel_ungated_paths).

evidence.reference: --dry-run validates item against extracted .items fragment, not whole schema; $ref-to-definitions item fails to resolve
same fragment-in-isolation validation pattern (latent — relations.schema has no $ref yet)
the canonical whole-file validateFromFile path the CLIs should route through (resolves $refs)
items {"$ref":"#/definitions/feature"} — the shape that breaks fragment validation; spec-reviews.schema.json:38 same

impact: file-block-item — the canonical append surface (CLAUDE.md filing pattern) — cannot append/preview/auto-id features (or any future $ref-item block kind), forcing a non-canonical raw appendToBlock tsx-eval fallback that bypasses the CLI's --writer/--dry-run/--auto-id ergonomics. Undermines the DEC-0019/0020 canonical-script surface for $ref-using schemas; the conception (samples) schemas may use $ref similarly, so .context filing would hit this too.
proposed_resolution: Both append CLIs should validate the candidate item by composing a whole-block wrapper and running validateFromFile against the FULL schema (mirroring writeBlock/appendToBlock) for --dry-run, NOT the item-fragment in isolation. Best: route --dry-run through the same library validation the real path uses (single path; eliminates the divergence). [CLOSED 2026-05-23 — commit 5ecd7bd / TASK-060 / VER-032: file-block-item + append-relation dry-run now validate the prospective WHOLE file (validateFromFile) not the items fragment — resolves $ref + dry-run==write parity.]
